title: keteleeria davidiana var. pubescens
intro: Keteleeria davidiana var. pubescens, also known as David's fir or pubescent David's fir, is a variant of the Keteleeria davidiana tree species, native to China and parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its small to medium size and distinctive pubescent leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Keteleeria davidiana var. pubescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve respiratory symptoms, and even have antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of this plant are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a decoction to harness its medicinal properties.
